sections.paragraph:
fut. יִסְפֹּר.-

(1) prop. i.q. Arab. شفر to scratch, to scrape (kindred to צָפַר No. II, ظفر); hence to polish (compare סַפִּיר, and the cogn. verb שָׁפַר).

(2) specially to inscribe letters on a stone; hence to write. Part. סֹפֵר a scribe, Psa 45:2 Ezr 9:2, Ezr 9:3specially-(a) the king’s scribe, i.e. the friend of the king, whose office it was to write his letters, 2Sa 8:17 20:25 2Ki 12:11 19:2 22:3 , seq.-(b) a military scribe, who has the charge of keeping the muster rolls, Jer 37:15 52:25 2Ki 25:19 compare 2Ch 26:11 Isa 33:18 gener. used of a general, Jud. 5:14 .-(c) in the later books, a person skilled in the sacred writings, γραμματεύς, 1Ch 27:32 Ezr 7:6, a name by which Ezra is called; Neh 8:1, seq.; 12:26 , 36 Neh 12:36 Ezr 7:11.

(3) to number, Gen 15:5 Lev 15:13, 28 Lev 15:28.

Niphal, pass. of Kal No. 3, to be numbered, Gen 16:10 32:13 .

Piel-

(1) i.q. Kal No. 3, to number, Job 38:37 Psa 40:6.

(2) to narrate, to recount (prop. to enumerate, compare Germ. zählen, erzählen), Gen 24:66 40:8 Job 28:27 followed by אֶל concerning any thing, Psa 2:7 69:27 especially to tell with praise, to celebrate, Psa 19:2 40:6 78:6 .

(3) simpl. to speak, to utter words, Psa 73:15 Isa 43:26.

Pual, סֻפַּר pass. of Piel No. 2, to be narrated, Psa 22:31 Job 37:20.

Derivatives, סְפֹורָה, סַפִּיר [מִסְפָּר], and the four following nouns-
